action.reasoning: Observation: The frame stacking and complex wrapper in iter 10 significantly slowed down training (took 3.66 hours) and reduced the success rate to 0.318. We only have 1.67 hours left in the time budget. Analysis: I need to revert to the simpler, faster architecture from iter 9 (which scored 0.54) to ensure the training finishes within the remaining time budget. I will use the embedding-based CNN without frame stacking and allocate a total of 10.5M timesteps across the environments, which should take about 1.2-1.4 hours. Decision: Rewrite wrapper.py, model.py, train.py, and predict.py to the iter 9 setup, run training, and submit for evaluation.
